title: Searching within messages — Full-text Search
author: UntypedLetter
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
The currently available search button 🔍 searches only the room names, not the message text.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I would like it to sift through all messages in all rooms for a particular search phrase.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I have considered using the Desktop Element just for searching.

title: Not receiving new message notification
author: UntypedLetter
body:
**Describe the bug**
When I send a message from Windows to Android, it does not display any notification on Android. Only when Schildi is opened, then it shows the green circle with 1 inside. Battery is set to unrestricted. Even while Schildi is open, new messages do not produce a banner notification, rather just a badge / green circle. A week or two ago, it was working perfectly, but then something happened. I have already run the notification troubleshooter and tests came correct. The notable thing is that the troubleshooter was able to produce a banner notification, yet newer messages were unable to produce it.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Send a message from Windows Element.
2. No banner notification is received on Schildi. But when you open Schildi, a green badge will be displayed.

**Expected behavior**
A banner notification should be triggered for every new message.

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - Device: Samsung Galaxy A03
 - OS: Android 13

**Additional context**
 - App version: 0.11.4(12100)
 - Store: Google Play
 - Homeserver: matrix.org
 - Related experimental/labs settings: No
 - Logs have been submitted using the app's built-in bug report (/"rageshake") mechanism: No

**Upstream relevance**
 - Does this issue exist in Element X Android?
I don't know since I am not using Element X.

title: Unread Message UI Elements for Threads Not Displaying Consistently
author: les2marmottes
body:
Describe the bug
Threaded messages trigger a push notification and increment the app badge correctly when the app is closed or in the background. However, once the app is opened, these unread messages are not indicated in the chat list. Specifically, they do not spawn a green dot next to the relevant chat (unlike non‑threaded unread messages), they do not contribute to the unread count of a space (at the bottom of the screen), and they are not included when filtering the chat list to show "Unread" rooms. This makes it nearly impossible to locate which chat contains the unread threaded message after opening the app based on the badge count.

To Reproduce

1. Receive a new message in a thread (within a room).
2. Observe that a push notification appears and the app badge increments.
3. Open the app without tapping the push notification.
4. Look at the chat list – no green dot appears next to the room containing the threaded message.
5. Apply the filter to show only "Unread" chats – the room with the threaded unread is not displayed.
6. Enable the "jump to unread" UI button (in SchildiChat) – this button does navigate correctly to the thread with the unread message, showing that the client internally knows about the unread state and its location.

Expected behavior
Unread messages in threads should behave consistently with unread non-thread messages. They should:
· Display a green dot next to the room in the chat list and contribute to the unread count shown next to space icons. 
· Cause the room to appear when filtering the chat list by "Unread".

Screenshots
(none relevant) 

Smartphone
Pixel 7
Android 16

Additional context
· App version: 0.11.4
· Store: Google Play
· Homeserver: matrix.org
· Related experimental/labs settings: Yes I have, and no it does not help! 
· Logs have been submitted via built‑in bug report: no. The bug report function keeps giving me an error and will not submit. 

Upstream relevance

· This issue also exists in Element X Android (observed on friends' iOS devices running Element X, so not specific to SchildiChat).
· If a corresponding bug report exists in Element X, please link it: https://github.com/element-hq/element-x-android/issues/7131

Additional observations
The "jump to unread" button in SchildiChat works correctly, confirming that the client has the needed unread state and location information internally, but it is not fully reflected in the main UI components (chat list dot, unread filter).

title: Add /discardsession command support
author: CommanderRedYT
body:

A simple way to reset a rooms crypto.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Having a button instead of the command would also work for me.

title: Opening the image begins to play automatically in a random order.
author: tony-studio
body:
**Describe the bug**
In an E2EE chat room, tapping any image opens the viewer. However, upon returning to the chat and reopening the same image, it immediately starts playing the images in a randomized order.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to 'any image'
2. Click on 'return'
3. Go to 'same image'
4. See error

**Expected behavior**
When viewing images, only the selected one is displayed; others will only appear when swiping left or right.

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: [Android 16]

**Additional context**
 - App version: [0.11.1-ex_26_4_4]
 - Store: [Google Play]

**Upstream relevance**
 - Does this issue exist in Element X Android?
 No.

title: Telegram-style bulk message selection
author: Kommynct
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Multi-message selection currently feels heavier and more disruptive than necessary. Entering selection mode changes the UI too aggressively and makes bulk actions on non-adjacent messages slower and less natural than they could be.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A Telegram-style message selection system:

* long pressing a message immediately selects it
* tapping additional messages toggles selection
* scrolling continues normally during selection
* the chat layout remains visually stable
* actions appear in a compact contextual toolbar

The important part is keeping selection lightweight and message-centric instead of feeling like a separate modal workflow.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
The current selection flow works functionally, but feels more cumbersome for large or non-adjacent selections. Checkbox-based or heavily modal approaches used by many apps also tend to interrupt conversational context and increase interaction cost.

**Additional context**
Telegram’s implementation is a good reference because it preserves spatial continuity and minimizes mode-switching. Selection feels like directly interacting with messages rather than entering a separate UI state, which makes bulk operations much faster and more comfortable.

title: Really hard to open the menu to react to a message with a mention split by text wrapping
author: ledlamp
body: **Describe the bug** Look at the video there was this message with a mention wrapped around making it almost impossible to touch and hold it to open the menu on it without touching the mention instead. **To Reproduce** Ig send a message like that try to break a mention in half with text wrapping. title: Feature Request: Support custom CAs (added to Android system pool)
author: little-quokka
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I really would like to update to the new schildichat for android, but it does not support custom certificate authorities. 

Details:
* My matrix server is a self-hosted conduit server that uses a credential signed by my own CA. 
* I manage my own CA with 'step ca'.
* I don't want to connect my instance to the internet and get a certificate by e.g., Let's Encrypt. 

I think this is a feature which will also be necessary for any company to make use of schildichat as they would usually manage their own CA as well. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
There are multiple possible solutions to this:
* network_security_config.xml
* interactive yes/no on first connect (identical to the old client)

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Alternatives are clunky:
* A reverse proxy at the edge of my network so that the client sees a Let's Encrypt cert. Issues: Hole in the firewall; Let's encrypt overhead; Mix of internal and external CAs;
* Build my own fork of this client. Issues: Hard to keep up with the fast development of this app. 

**Additional context**
N/A

title: Wide images sent via webhooks/HTML payloads render as cropped
author: bl4ckswordsman
body:
**Describe the bug** When sending wide format images via webhooks/bots with the img tag, the rendered images show as cropped. **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Upload a wide format image to the matrix server and note the mxc:// url. 2. Send a payload with the image, something like: ```json { "text": "", "html": "300x100 sample image, with width=300 tag:
" } ``` 3. See the rendered image, which will be cropped into a square-ish format. **Expected behavior** The rendered image should retain its ratio, and not be cropped but preferably scaled (if necessary) **Screenshots** SchildiChat Next: Element Web: For reference, this is how it shows in another Android matrix client, FluffyChat: **Smartphone (please complete the following information):** - Device: Pixel - OS: Android 16 **Additional context** - App version: 0.11.0 - Store: SpiritCroc's F-Droid - Homeserver: self-hosted - Related experimental/labs settings: Link previews, Disabled Legacy renderer - Logs have been submitted using the app's built-in bug report (/"rageshake") mechanism: no **Upstream relevance** Element X doesn't even render images.

title: Show `body` instead of `formatted_body` in notification text
author: bl4ckswordsman
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Since discussions are disabled on this repo, posting here instead, mainly trying to understand if this would be too complex to implement and how it works.

Sometimes (as I've discovered playing around with some bot/webhooks), the `content.body` value in message jsons can be shorter and more appropriate than `content.formatted_body` as the notification text (i.e. the text that shows in a push notification/pop up when you receive a new message).
This is actually how it works in Element Web and Element Desktop, it always shows the `body` text as the notification text.

In SC Next and Element X it seems that it only shows the `body` text in the notification when there's no `formatted_body` in the message json.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I'm not sure if it can and how it would be implemented, but a solution would be a behavior similar to how notification text shows up in Element Web/Desktop.

title: User mentions displayed show full "@user:domain.com" instead of just "@user"
author: bl4ckswordsman
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
In Element X, if I mention someone, it displays as `@user` but in SchildiChat Next it displays as `@user:domain.com`.
A workaround is to enable 'Legacy message renderer' but that creates other display problems as it lacks some formatting options.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I think it would be nice if this particular aspect (user mentions) matched Element X. I'm guessing it could be achieved with some sort of adjustment to SchildiChat Next's MXID-to-displayname resolution.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
N/A

**Additional context**

title: Support notification setting per room / user
author: tarminu
body:
I would like to have unique notification sounds for specific rooms and users so I know if a new message is something I should look at sooner or if it is low priority and can wait.

I would like to be able to specify the notification sound, priority, lock screen visibility, and other Android notification parameters as needed for the users and rooms I specify.

title: Avatars missing for bridged Signal direct chats (mautrix-signal)
author: mr8tom
body: **Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.** When using **mautrix-signal** bridge with `use_contact_avatars: true`, contact avatars are not displayed in bridged Signal 1:1 (direct) chats. All other Bridges (Whatsapp, Telegram, Instagram) show the avatars. Maybe it's not . Btw, I like your Idea of putting the Bridge-Icon as small Icon next to the Avatar!! I testet it with the other apps in comparisson if the Avatar can be seen: - Element: Yes, Avatar can be seen - SchildiChat Next (Android): Nope - SchildiChat Desktop (Alpha): Yes, Avatar can be seen - SchildiChat Desktop (Revenge): Nope **Describe the solution you'd like** I want Avatars ^^ **Describe alternatives you've considered** It works if I force the room to use a custom avatar (upload jpg) **Additional context** Maybe my config is shitty? private_chat_portal_meta: true forces to use avatar + name over bridge ``` vars.yml: matrix_mautrix_signal_configuration_extension_yaml: | bridge: private_chat_portal_meta: true matrix: sync_direct_chat_list: true delivery_receipts: true ```
